description:
Fix documentation that references the non-working `npx create-opentabs-plugin` command, and add resource/prompt scaffold examples to improve first-time plugin developer experience.

Context: QA testing in a clean Docker environment confirmed that `npx create-opentabs-plugin` returns a 404 error because npm resolves by package name (the package is `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin`, not `create-opentabs-plugin`). Only `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` or `opentabs plugin create` work. Multiple docs files still reference the broken command.

Additionally, the scaffold generates only tools — no resource or prompt examples. When developers add resources or prompts, they encounter TS4114 errors requiring `override` with no scaffold example to follow. The plugin-development docs explain this, but a commented hint in the generated index.ts would prevent the confusion entirely.

Known LOW issues (not creating stories for these):
- The plugin-development.mdx code examples use double quotes which would fail the project's own prettier rules. This is cosmetic since docs code examples don't get linted.
- `npx opentabs-plugin build` outside a plugin dir gives a 404 because the binary name doesn't match the npm package name (`@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools`). This is a structural npm naming limitation that can't be trivially fixed.

userStories.title: Fix npx create-opentabs-plugin references in root docs
userStories.description: As a first-time plugin developer, I want the documented scaffold commands to actually work so that I don't get a confusing 404 error when following the instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md no longer contains `npx create-opentabs-plugin` — replaced with `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` (or `opentabs plugin create` as the primary recommendation)
CLAUDE.md no longer contains `npx create-opentabs-plugin` — replaced with the working command
plugins/README.md no longer contains `npx create-opentabs-plugin` — replaced with the working command
No other .md or .mdx files in the repo reference the non-working `npx create-opentabs-plugin` command (search the entire repo to verify)
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- `README.md` line ~106: `npx create-opentabs-plugin my-plugin` → `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin my-plugin --domain .example.com`
- `CLAUDE.md` line ~129: `npx create-opentabs-plugin` → `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` (or recommend `opentabs plugin create` as the primary path)
- `plugins/README.md` line ~37: `npx create-opentabs-plugin <name> --domain <domain>` → `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin <name> --domain <domain>`

Also search for any other references: `grep -r 'create-opentabs-plugin' --include='*.md' --include='*.mdx' .` (excluding CHANGELOG.md which is historical).

The recommended primary command should be `opentabs plugin create` since that's bundled with the CLI. The `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` form should be shown as an alternative for developers who don't have the CLI installed globally.

userStories.title: Add resource and prompt scaffold hints to generated index.ts
userStories.description: As a first-time plugin developer, I want the scaffolded index.ts to include commented-out examples for resources and prompts so that I can see the correct syntax (including `override`) without hunting through documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: The generated `src/index.ts` includes a commented block after the `tools` property showing how to add resources with `override readonly resources: ResourceDefinition[]`
The generated `src/index.ts` includes a commented block showing how to add prompts with `override readonly prompts: PromptDefinition[]`
The comments mention the `override` keyword is required and reference the Resources & Prompts docs page
The `ResourceDefinition` and `PromptDefinition` types are included in the import statement (in the `import type` line)
The generated code still passes lint after `npm run lint:fix` (comments must use single quotes if they contain string examples)
